l1: Are clients across all sites failing to locate a local domain controller?
What specific error messages are thrown during client join or logon attempts?
Is the issue limited to a particular subnet, VLAN, or geographical site location?
l2: Are the AD Site and Subnet mapping assignments correctly configured in ADSS?
Are DNS server IPs pointing correctly to local or corporate domain DNS zones?
Are firewalls dropping UDP port 389 LDAP ping calls used during discovery?

l1: Run 'nltest /dsgetdc:<domain>' from the client to test locator process.
Run 'nltest /whowasi' to identify the last contacted domain controller entity.
l2: Run 'dsregcmd /status' to review discovery data blocks on hybrid joined systems.
Check Netlogon logs on client for specific locator failure execution records.
Verify local subnet mapping validity using 'nltest /dsaddress:<IP>' logic.

l1: Flush the local client resolver cache using 'ipconfig /flushdns'.
Correct the DNS client settings to target valid Active Directory integrated servers.
Restart Netlogon service on domain controllers to force SRV registrations.
l2: Add the missing client subnet range into the Active Directory Sites and Services configuration.
Resolve corporate firewall rule configurations to permit UDP 389 and TCP 3268 flows.
Fix broken DNS delegation records pointing toward parent infrastructure zones.

l1: Collect text files containing outputs of nltest discovery commands.
Export the complete ipconfig configurations and local hosts configuration files.
Gather outputs of nslookup queries targeting the root domain controllers.
l2: Enable Netlogon logging on the client by creating %windir%\debug\netlogon.log.
Capture wire packet traces during execution of 'nltest /dsgetdc' queries.
Export the Active Directory site-to-subnet mapping registry tables summary.

srv_records_missing.title: Client DC Locator fails due to missing DNS SRV Records

steps.expect: Check if records exist; if output returns non-existent domain, DNS is missing SRV registration
Forces registration of local DC records, or manual restart of the Netlogon service
Successfully returns target DC name, IP address, and associated Active Directory site info
